criteria.description: All dev-phase tasks have reached a terminal status (completed or dlq). DLQ is accepted-terminal for dev (retries exhausted) and surfaced via evidence.dlq_blocks_criterion; critical-tier DLQ is escalated by the meta-orchestrator (E04). Contrast: the test phase blocks on DLQ.
Every delivery.md artifact from completed dev tasks contains qa_ready: true
No delivery.md artifact contains a non-empty prohibition_violations list

SIEGARD-04 — after dev, HEAD is on the integration branch (main), the working tree is clean, no feat/fix/refactor TC branch remains unmerged, and no per-TC worktree remains. The Orchestrator-Dev merges every qa_ready TC branch into main and removes worktrees before transition so QA (review) runs on the integrated head.

Rec #11 — every completed dev task's delivery-gate acceptance_criteria block is present with an empty uncovered list and covered == total. Independent gate that surfaces AC coverage gaps at end of dev instead of letting them slip silently into review.

Rec A — every UC/FEAT defined in a spec the backlog references is covered by at least one Task Contract (origin/bdd_ref/objective). Blocks dev exit when the planner silently under-scoped a requirement. Enforced only for standard/greenfield flows; improve/synthesized backlogs are out of scope (subset by design). BR coverage is informational, not blocking.
